config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent Indonesia vs China native-or-English block. Each persona speaks only English or its own native language.
config.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: SAYA TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap menjadi batas yang tidak boleh dikorbankan begitu saja atas nama kepentingan masyarakat, karena kalau tidak, mudah sekali terjadi penyalahgunaan oleh mayoritas atau penguasa. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai dengan cara yang tetap menghormati martabat dan kebebasan tiap orang. Dalam pandangan saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun ketika hak individu dilindungi, bukan ditenggelamkan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should matter a lot, but they should not automatically take priority over the rights of the individual. From my perspective as someone from China, I understand why many people put social stability and collective welfare first, because a society can become chaotic very quickly if everyone only defends personal freedom without responsibility. But I also think that when “society first” becomes an excuse to silence dissent or ignore basic rights, it creates abuse and fear instead of real harmony. A healthy society should balance the two: protect individual rights strongly, while asking individuals to accept reasonable limits when the public interest is genuinely at stake.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan di atas hak individu secara umum. Dari pengalaman melihat Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan bersama” sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang terlalu jauh, padahal yang benar justru negara dan masyarakat harus melindungi hak dasar dulu. Kepentingan publik memang penting, tetapi boleh mengalah pada hak individu hanya dalam batas yang benar-benar ketat, jelas, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan. Kalau tidak, yang terjadi bukan harmoni, melainkan mayoritas atau penguasa lebih mudah menekan orang kecil.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not automatically outrank individual rights, because that is exactly how abuse gets justified. From my perspective as someone from China, I understand the appeal of social order and the concern that too much individualism can weaken responsibility, but I also know that “for the public good” can become a very convenient slogan for suppressing dissent or protecting power. In real life, I think the burden should be on society and the state to prove that any limit on rights is necessary, narrow, and transparent, not just assumed to be legitimate. A stable society is stronger when people feel their basic rights are secure, not when they have to fear being sacrificed for vague collective goals.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu sebagai prinsip umum. Dari konteks Indonesia, saya melihat alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang berlebihan, jadi saya tidak percaya pada klaim itu kalau tidak ada batas yang sangat jelas. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi justru harus dijaga dengan melindungi hak dasar dulu, bukan mengorbankannya lebih dulu. Kalau batasnya kabur, yang paling sering rugi adalah orang kecil, bukan masyarakat secara keseluruhan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not be treated as automatically more important than the rights of the individual. From my perspective as someone from China, I understand why people value order, stability, and collective goals, because a society with no discipline can become chaotic very quickly, but I have also seen how easily “for the common good” can be used to excuse unfair treatment or silence ordinary people. I do not accept the idea that the collective should simply override basic rights; that is too dangerous and too vague. A good society should protect individual rights first as a baseline, and only limit them in narrow, transparent, and truly necessary cases.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
